qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: call_user_func_array() allows code injection through array callback method names
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets `src/php/funchand/call_user_func_array.js` and its `eval(cb[0] + "['" + cb[1] + "']")` injection path, not `src/php/strings/parse_str.js` and the advisory's `String.prototype.includes` blacklist bypass that enables prototype pollution.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Generated tests execute attacker-controlled header example lines
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets `src/_util/util.ts` test-generation logic that emits header comment lines into Vitest files, not `src/php/strings/parse_str.js` and the advisory's `constructor[prototype]` prototype-pollution bypass through overridden `String.prototype.includes`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable `parse_str` code path or its root cause: a prototype-pollution guard bypass caused by trusting `String.prototype.includes` on attacker-influenced keys.
evidence: The advisory describes `src/php/strings/parse_str.js` lines 77-79, where `key.includes('__proto__') || key.includes('constructor') || key.includes('prototype')` can be bypassed after overriding `String.prototype.includes`, allowing `constructor[prototype][...]` input to pollute `Object.prototype`.
`call_user_func_array_eval_rce` instead reports eval-based code injection in `src/php/funchand/call_user_func_array.js` via attacker-controlled array callback method names.
`generated_test_header_rce` instead reports build-time code execution in `src/_util/util.ts` when header comment lines are copied into generated Vitest files.

The preserved session history only shows a simpler `parse_str('x[__proto__][polluted]=1&safe=ok', ...)` check returning `{} clean`; no final finding or reproduced PoC exercises the advisory's `String.prototype.includes` bypass or demonstrates `Object.prototype` pollution through `constructor[prototype]` input.
